headline: MPs' constituency allocation lacks any spending guidelines or audit
context: Each MP receives RM3-5 million annually in constituency development funds with no standardised spending guidelines, no audit requirement, and no public reporting mechanism.

cards.big: 222 MPs share RM1.1 billion in constituency funds — with zero audit requirements
cards.sub: Public money flows to constituencies without any accountability mechanism

cards.big: No legislation governs constituency fund allocation, spending, or reporting
cards.sub: Funds operate in a legal vacuum — neither appropriated nor audited systematically

cards.big: Government MPs receive RM5 million while opposition MPs get RM3 million or less
cards.sub: Funding disparity punishes voters for their electoral choice

cards.big: Philippines' pork barrel scandal led to Supreme Court abolishing discretionary MP funds
cards.sub: Similar systems elsewhere have been found unconstitutional and corruption-prone

cards.big: Unaudited constituency funds are the most legal form of political patronage in Malaysia
Should constituency funds be equalised and subjected to Auditor General review?
